references.title: Affective States and State Tests: Investigating How Affect and Engagement during the School Year Predict End-of-Year Learning Outcomes

extracted.Data sources: Labelling of Affect and Behavior Observations, LMS logs,
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Labelling of Affect and Behavior observation was performed by 2 experts by combining field observations with

annotations.text: Authors hypothesize that there is a relationship between student affect, engagement and their learning outcomes. Authors look into ASSISTments data and found boredom during exercises to be negatively correlated with performance. However, boredom during a tutoring session was positively correlated with performance.
On a second experiment, authors use affect and disengaged behavior on the tutoring system to predict performance. Model achieves good correlation with standardized exam scores.
